Younger adults are ageing faster than generations before them, making them more susceptible to cancer , a new study has found. Earlier this year, it was revealed that 11 cancers are now rising among adults aged 20 to 49 - breast, bowel, thyroid, oral, endometrial, liver, kidney, gallbladder, pancreatic, womb and ovarian. Cancer has traditionally been more commonly diagnosed in older people, leaving doctors puzzled.

Ticket reselling is just scalping by another name. In the UK, ticket sales is a total racket, "resellers" use bots to hoover up tickets which they resell at a VAST profit when they first become available. Both "resellers" and venues are notorious for also holding onto a substantial number of tickets and selling them closer to the show date for HUGE mark-ups. This needs to stop, but UK Trading Standards seem unable (or unwilling??) to do anything.

Supply and Demand - How long have the bar managers in Boston known that the Scots like to drink before and after a game ? Planning is all - Cheers !

England fans have descended on Boston chanting 'we'll drink all your beer' - only to discover thirsty Scotland supporters have left the city almost completely dry. Pubs have already run out of ale after a week of hosting the Tartan Army, with even emergency resupply missions failing to keep pace with demand. As Scottish supporters gradually depart for their next game in Miami, many thousands of England fans are piling into Boston ahead of their second group game tonight against Ghana.
  • 50 comments
  • 1 video
  • share England fans arrive in US to find Tartan Army has drunk beer supplies

I really must be getting old if my first take on this is - first, grabbing someone by the arm is assault these days? Second, Shouldn't do it, but that was enough to destroy a high flying career and an adult lifetime of training and building expertise??

A top City lawyer who lost his career after his wife accused him of assault has been cleared by a judge - after she was found to have 'embellished' the details. Solicitor Boris Telyatnikov, 42, denied grabbing the arm of pharmaceutical executive Sophie Voigt-Firon while demanding she transfer £4,000 to his account. Voigt-Firon had claimed her then-husband arrived home drunk on July 21 last year at their five-bedroom home in Surrey Lane, Battersea. But privately-educated Telyatnikov, the son of former Soviet Union diplomats, was found not guilty of assault during a trial at Wimbledon Magistrates' Court last week. The court heard intimate details of the couple's troubled marriage - with District Judge Rebecca Saillet saying Voigt-Firon had previously 'lied to the defendant over the course of their relationship'.
  • 44 comments
  • share Top City lawyer cleared of assaulting his wife during money row

If you can’t access it it’s dermatillomania. Also known as skin picking disorder, this is a condition where someone will pick at spots, scars and moles to try to smooth the skin, whether with fingers, nails, teeth, tweezers or pins. It's not always conscious and can often flare up when stressed or anxious. 

Professional make-up artist Beth grew up with great skin. Even in her teens, she was blessed with a flawless complexion with no breakouts. But in her late twenties everything changed.

EXCLUSIVEGladiators star Giant splits from his OnlyFans model girlfriend Taylor Ryan just weeks after he was axed by the BBC over their relationship

Gladiators star Giant has split from his OnlyFans model girlfriend two months after the relationship cost him his job. The recent divorcee, 40, was sacked from the show in April after being confronted about his closeness to Taylor Ryan, 28, by BBC bosses. The pair - who remain friends - have now split and Giant - real name Jamie Bigg - has travelled to Spain for a period of reflection.
